commit.message:
perf: eliminate unnecessary rendering work for zero-alpha and none fills

- PathDrawer: replace regex/split path tokenizer with zero-allocation
  char-scanner (PathScanner inner class). Avoids PATH_LETTER_PATTERN
  replaceAll, normalize() calls, and WHITESPACE.split() per render.

- Colors.color(): dispatch by string prefix instead of trying all regex
  patterns in sequence. Hex colors (#rrggbb/#rgb) now bypass all Matchers
  entirely; rgb()/rgba()/hsl()/hsla() only create Matchers for matching
  prefixes. Eliminates up to 5 wasted Matcher allocations per hex color.

- Helpers.paint(): skip PAINT_URL regex for non-url() values; check
  startsWith("url") before creating the Matcher.

- Surface.draw(): skip context.fill(path) when fill is "none" or fully
  transparent. Previously Java2D's Marlin rasterizer ran the full path
  for every fill="none" element. For SVGs heavy with stroke-only paths
  (3 of 6 in the complex_paths benchmark) this saves one full fill
  rasterization per path element per render.

Render-only benchmark improvement for complex_paths: 0.486ms -> 0.379ms
(~22% faster). JairoSVG now within 5% of JSVG on complex_paths full
benchmark (was 40% gap in render-only mode before these changes).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(v0.6.2): security hardening + fleet parity docs + hygiene test suite

- Add 24-test security_hygiene integration test suite (TC-SEC-01..24):
  session-name validation, no sh -c with user input, 0o600 tempfile
  permissions, no hardcoded secrets, shell metachar rejection
- fleet.rs: add validate_session_name() invariant doc comment; use

  update fleet-command.md, use-fleet-dashboard.md, index.md

All 205 fleet + 31 no_python_probe + 24 security_hygiene tests pass.
cargo fmt --check: clean. cargo clippy -- -D warnings: zero warnings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(issue-77): memory-layer backend-migration groundwork

Lays the seam for future backend migrations (e.g. LadybugDB) by extracting
all SQLite memory operations into a symmetric backend module, fixing Kùzu
backend correctness bugs, and adding a comprehensive parity test suite.

## Changes

### backend/sqlite.rs (new)
- `SqliteBackend` struct implementing all three backend traits
  (`MemoryTreeBackend`, `MemorySessionBackend`, `MemoryRuntimeBackend`)
- All SQLite-specific code extracted from `memory/mod.rs`
- Symmetric with `backend/kuzu.rs` — single insertion point for future backends

### backend/kuzu.rs (fixed)
- `load_session_rows` now honours the `memory_type: Option<&str>` filter
  (previously ignored — would return all memory types regardless)
- `query_kuzu_memories_for_session` skips tables whose normalised label
  doesn't match the requested type; applies Rust-side expiry filter
- `collect_kuzu_agent_counts` uses per-row expiry checks for tables with
  `has_expires_at=true`, matching SQLite's WHERE clause semantics
- `KUZU_MEMORY_TABLES` extended to `&[(&str, &str, bool)]` — bool marks
  tables that carry an `expires_at` column

### memory/mod.rs (cleaned)
- SQLite-specific helpers removed (now live in `backend/sqlite.rs`)
- `parse_memory_timestamp` made `pub(crate)` and extended with a new
  branch for Kùzu's `time::OffsetDateTime` Display format
  (`"YYYY-MM-DD H:MM:SS.f +HH:MM:SS"`)

### backend/memory_backend_parity_test.rs (new)
- 14 tests exercising every trait method against both backends:
  runtime round-trip, duplicate dedup, session-id filter, memory-type
  filter (episodic vs semantic), agent-count aggregation, list/delete
  session, expiry exclusion

## Test results
- `cargo test --workspace`: 561 passed, 0 failed
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`: 0 errors
- `cargo fmt --check`: clean

Closes #77 (backend migration groundwork portion)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(issue-77): enforce backend-neutral env contract + harden path validation

Completes the memory-layer backend-migration groundwork by enforcing
the backend-neutral environment contract in child processes and hardening
path validation against unsafe overrides.

## Changes

### env_builder.rs
- Add `removed_vars: HashSet<String>` to EnvBuilder for explicit env removals
- Add `unset()` method to mark variables for removal from child processes
- Add `apply_to_command()` method that atomically applies both sets and removals
- `with_project_graph_db` now removes `AMPLIHACK_KUZU_DB_PATH` from child env
  so children only see the backend-neutral `AMPLIHACK_GRAPH_DB_PATH` contract

### fleet.rs
- Replace `load_failed = true` silent fallbacks with `bail!()` in task queue
  and dashboard JSON load failures (no silent warn-and-continue)
- Replace `unwrap_or_default()` on TOML parse with propagating error + backup

### memory/backend/kuzu.rs
- Add `validate_graph_db_override()` inside `resolve_memory_graph_db_path()`
  rejecting non-absolute, parent-traversal, and /proc|/sys|/dev prefixed paths

### memory/mod.rs
- Add three new path-validation tests covering relative override rejection,
  /proc prefix rejection, and KUZU fallback cascade when GRAPH override is invalid
- Remove now-unused imports (kuzu_f64, kuzu_i64, kuzu_string) from public use

### launch.rs / recipe/run.rs
- Switch from `.build()` + `.envs()` to `.apply_to_command()` so env removals
  (AMPLIHACK_KUZU_DB_PATH) are propagated correctly to child processes

### memory/transfer/backend.rs / code_graph.rs
- Minor import and path hygiene aligned with backend-neutral terminology

All 570 tests pass; cargo clippy -D warnings clean; cargo fmt --check clean.
No amploxy files modified.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(issue-77): neutralize legacy kuzu names in public API + harden path validation

- Rename `KuzuBackend` → `GraphDbBackend` (kuzu.rs) — the struct is an
  implementation detail; the graph-db abstraction is the public contract
- Rename `open_kuzu_memory_db` → `open_graph_db_memory_db`
- Rename `collect_kuzu_agent_counts` → `collect_graph_db_agent_counts`
- Expose neutral aliases in `ffi_test_support`: `init_graph_backend_schema`,
  `graph_rows`, `list_graph_sessions_from_conn` (old names removed)
- Update `kuzu_ffi_test.rs` to use the new neutral aliases; all 31 FFI tests
  still pass
- Harden `validate_graph_db_override`: return `Result` instead of `Option`
  so bad AMPLIHACK_GRAPH_DB_PATH / AMPLIHACK_KUZU_DB_PATH values produce
  actionable errors instead of silent falls-through
- Refactor `store_learning_sqlite` → `store_learning_sqlite_with_conn`:
  accepts a pre-opened connection; collapses INSERT-OR-IGNORE + UPDATE into
  a single UPSERT per table (SQLite 3.24+ ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE)
- Micro-optimise `memory_relevance_score`: pre-compute query word-set at
  call site once rather than per-record; use byte-len / 4 token approximation
  (O(1) vs O(n chars))
- Single clock read in `store_learning_kuzu_with_conn`: derive RFC3339 from
  the same OffsetDateTime used for Kuzu TIMESTAMP parameters

All 615+ unit tests + 31 integration FFI tests pass.

commit.message:
perf: revert mask loop to combined pass with BT.709 luminance

TYPE_BYTE_GRAY was rejected: Java2D uses BT.601 coefficients (0.587*G)
for sRGB→gray conversion, producing gray=150 for pure green, while
SVG spec requires BT.709 (0.7152*G = 182). The difference is ~17%
and breaks the mask luminance correctness test.

Revert to the original combined source+mask pixel loop, but now it
operates on the sub-region buffer (from the previous commit) which is
typically 5x smaller than the full image. Added a fast path for fully
opaque white mask pixels (luminance=255, alpha=255) which are the
dominant case for solid-white mask fills.

Mask render-only benchmark (15_masks.svg, 3000 iterations):
  Before any changes: JairoSVG 1.818ms  JSVG 1.139ms  (+60%)
  Sub-region only:    JairoSVG 1.236ms  JSVG 1.038ms  (+19%)
  Combined loop:      JairoSVG 1.018ms  JSVG 1.064ms  (-4%, JairoSVG now faster)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add .temp to gitignore

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: optimize mask rendering — sub-region + DST_IN composite

Two changes targeting the 60% mask rendering gap vs JSVG:

1. Remove mask bbox expansion in Surface.draw()
   The sub-region effect buffer was being expanded to include the full
   mask content bounding box, which caused the optimization to be
   disabled for any mask whose content spans the full viewport (the
   common case). Drop this expansion — the existing 4px padding and the
   sub-region transform already ensure correct rendering of mask shapes
   near element boundaries.
   Result: element sub-regions now activate for masked nodes (e.g. a
   180×130 element uses a 23K-pixel buffer instead of 120K-pixel).

2. Replace dual-array pixel loop in paintMask() with DST_IN composite
   Instead of iterating source + mask pixels simultaneously, convert
   mask pixels to luminance-as-alpha in a single mask-only pass, then
   let Graphics2D.AlphaComposite.DstIn apply the result to the source
   image. This halves memory bandwidth in the hot loop and offloads
   the final blend to the JDK's optimized compositor.

Combined effect on 15_masks.svg render-only benchmark:
  Before: 1.818 ms/iter  (JairoSVG 60% slower than JSVG)
  After:  1.236 ms/iter  (JairoSVG 19% slower than JSVG)
  Improvement: ~32%

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: reduce per-render allocations and redundant string parsing

- Pre-allocated AffineTransform stack (depth 64) in Surface.draw() replaces
  per-node context.getTransform() allocations; reuse stack slot for sub-region
  effect compositing instead of new AffineTransform()
- Cache FontRenderContext on Surface after rendering hints setup; TextDrawer
  reads cachedFRC instead of calling getFontRenderContext() every text node
- Gradient stop cache (IdentityHashMap<Node, GradientStops>) in Surface;
  Defs.drawGradient() factors out fractions/colors construction from both
  linearGradient and radialGradient branches, caches result keyed by node
  identity and opacity
- Node cache fields for parsed path (cachedPath/cachedVertices), transform
  string (cachedTransform/cachedTransformStr), and text coords
  (cachedXCoords/cachedYCoords/cachedDxCoords/cachedDyCoords)
- PathDrawer.path() checks cachedPath on entry and stores result on exit;
  avoids re-parsing 'd' attribute on repeated renders (use, markers)
- Helpers.transform() 5-arg overload uses node.cachedTransform when transform
  string is unchanged and no gradient/origin involved; original 4-arg overload
  unchanged for Defs gradient transform handling
- TextDrawer caches split results for x/y/dx/dy coord strings on node

refactor: sharpen all 14 skill descriptions with precision-trigger technical terms

Replace generic 'Load when working with X' descriptions with surgical triggers
that reference specific Java annotations, error messages, Kubernetes resources,
and Spring Boot concepts that naturally appear in conversations when each skill
is relevant.

Changes per skill:
- jpa-patterns: triggers on @Entity, @Repository, LazyInitializationException, @EntityGraph, N+1
- springboot-patterns: triggers on @Service, @Async, @Cacheable, @RestControllerAdvice, @Transactional
- springboot-security: triggers on SecurityFilterChain, JwtAuthenticationFilter, @PreAuthorize, CORS, CSRF
- springboot-tdd: triggers on @WebMvcTest, @DataJpaTest, @MockBean, Testcontainers, TDD red-green-refactor
- springboot-verification: triggers on jacoco:report, Checkstyle, SpotBugs, dependency-check:check, GitHub Actions
- api-design: triggers on HTTP methods, RFC 7807 ProblemDetail, @Operation, cursor pagination, HATEOAS
- database-migrations: triggers on V{n}__.sql, Flyway, Liquibase, expand-contract DDL, FlywayException
- docker-patterns: triggers on Dockerfile, FROM eclipse-temurin, -XX:MaxRAMPercentage, layertools
- deployment-patterns: triggers on K8s probes, /actuator/health/liveness, blue-green, HPA, graceful shutdown
- e2e-testing: triggers on REST Assured, Testcontainers singleton, WireMock, Spring Cloud Contract
- postgres-patterns: triggers on EXPLAIN ANALYZE, HikariCP, GIN index, pg_stat_activity, PARTITION BY
- search-first: triggers on 'implement', 'create a', 'add a', 'write a method for'
- iterative-retrieval: triggers on large codebases, context overflow, multi-module Maven
- continuous-learning: triggers on 'write an ADR', 'document this pattern', 'this keeps coming up'

commit.message:
Silently handle SDK delete failures for disk-only sessions

The SDK doesn't track sessions that have no events.jsonl, so its
delete call fails with 'session file not found'. Since disk delete
and UI removal always succeed regardless, just swallow the SDK
error silently instead of logging a confusing 'failed' message.

fix(observer): deduplicate subscriptions in ObserverSubject (RI-015)

Subscribing the same callback reference twice would cause double
notifications per notify() call. Added an includes() guard before
push so duplicate registrations are silently ignored, matching the
DualObserverSubject pattern (filter-based unsubscribe already removed
all occurrences there).

Also adds a test verifying that subscribing the same callback twice
results in exactly one notification and one observer registered.

Updates ROADMAP.md with RI-011 through RI-015 from this validation pass.
